_comment: AzkaSocial question bank — extracted and adapted from 'GMS Workbook: Language Arts, Upper Elementary, Grade 4 Term 1' (Green Montessori School). Chapters follow the workbook's table of contents order. Each chapter has a large shuffleable pool; the game randomly picks 5 per playthrough and rotates question types.

chapters.title: Correct the Spelling Mistake
chapters.snippet: Even tricky words like 'ordinary' and 'important' follow patterns once you spot the mistake!

questions.prompt: Fix the spelling mistake: "George offtin makes a mess at dinner time." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "I hurd you playing from the other room!" What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Eating froot is part of a balanced diet." What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the word meaning "a set of people or things"?
Fix the spelling mistake: "My rabbit is not very ordineree; she hates eating carrots." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "I love to lern about different planets." What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the word meaning "to construct"?
Fix the spelling mistake: "We will concidur everyone's entries before choosing a winner." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Earlier, there were some straynj noises coming from our shed." What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the organ that pumps blood?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Taj was worried when he had to take an impawtent test." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "We have been told to aryv at exactly half past two." What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the planet we live on?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Sometimes, the road near our school can be very bizzy." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Did you notis the new classroom display about dragons?" What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the word meaning "the one you like best"?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Last week, we held a surpryz birthday party for my nan." What is the correct word?
Fix the spelling mistake: "Hopefully, Jenna will enjoy her new bysikle." What is the correct word?
Which is the correct spelling of the word meaning "to picture in your mind"?
Fix the spelling mistake: "I couldn't beleev it when they said that my sister had won." What is the correct word?
Match each misspelled word to its correct spelling.

questions.example: My rabbit is not very ordinary; she hates eating carrots.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.

chapters.title: Antonyms Are Opposites
chapters.snippet: Rosa Parks stood up for what was fair and just — antonyms like 'unfair' and 'fair' help us describe the world around us.

questions.prompt: Match each word from the Rosa Parks story to its antonym (opposite).
Match each word from the Rosa Parks story to its antonym (opposite).
Complete with an antonym: "The fire is hot, but the ice is ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The race car is fast, but the old truck is ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The green light means go, but the red light means ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The grandfather is old, but the grandchild is ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The candy is sweet, but the lemon is ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The window is closed, but the door is ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The children are noisy, but the adults are ___."
Complete with an antonym: "The rainforest is humid, but the desert is ___."
"I walked to the shop quickly." Which word is the antonym of 'quickly'?
"The garden was beautiful in the summer." What is the antonym of 'beautiful'?
"My phone's battery is full." What is the antonym of 'full'?
"Mum was angry when I got home." What is the antonym of 'angry'?
"I picked the largest sweet from the bowl." What is the antonym of 'largest'?
"The film was so sad." What is the antonym of 'sad'?
"Football is so exciting." What is the antonym of 'exciting'?
"My teacher hates rainy days." What is the antonym of 'hates'?

questions.definition: A word that means the opposite of another word.
questions.example: The lion is wild, but the cat is tame — wild and tame are antonyms.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: The lion is wild but the cat is tame

chapters.title: Prefixes and Suffixes
chapters.snippet: Adding 'un-' or '-less' to a word can flip its whole meaning — like turning 'happy' into 'unhappy'!

questions.prompt: The prefix 're-' means...
The prefix 'bi-' means...
The prefix 'un-' means...
Complete using the prefix un-: "My shoe is ___." (not tied)
Complete using the prefix dis-: "Carl ___ with Paul." (does not agree)
Complete using the prefix un-: "Abby is ___ to beat me in the video game." (not able)
Complete using the prefix dis-: "Will ___ his parents." (does not obey)
The suffix '-ful' means...
The suffix '-less' means...
Complete using the suffix -ful: "I am ___ to have such a loving family." (full of thanks)
Complete using the suffix -less: "Will saw a ___ puppy wandering around town." (without a home)
Complete using the suffix -ful: "Carla, you look ___ in that dress." (full of beauty)
Complete using the suffix -less: "The squirrels in the backyard are ___." (without harm)
Match each prefix to its meaning.
Match each suffix to its meaning.

questions.definition: A group of letters added to the beginning of a word that changes its meaning.
questions.example: Adding 're-' to 'turn' makes 'return' — to turn again.

questions.definition: A group of letters added to the end of a word that changes its meaning.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.

chapters.title: Contractions
chapters.snippet: Squishing two words into one, like 'do not' into 'don't', is called a contraction — don't forget the apostrophe!

questions.prompt: "I'll" is short for...
"You'll" is short for...
"It's" is short for...
"Wouldn't" is short for...
"Couldn't" is short for...
"Shouldn't" is short for...
"Haven't" is short for...
"You'd" is short for...
Which is the correct contraction for "he will"?
Which is the correct contraction for "they will"?
Fix the spelling: "carnt" should be written as...
Fix the spelling: "didnt" should be written as...
Fix the spelling: "cudn't" should be written as...
Fix the spelling: "wudn't" should be written as...
Fix the spelling: "woul'dve" should be written as...
Match each contraction to its full form.
Match each contraction to its full form.

questions.definition: A shortened form of a word or group of words, with an apostrophe marking the missing letters.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.

chapters.title: Capitalization and Punctuation
chapters.snippet: Every sentence needs a capital letter to start and a full stop to finish — like tucking it in at both ends!

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "mark and carl got wet in the rain"
questions.answer: Mark and Carl got wet in the rain.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "jill has fair hair but jack has dark hair"
questions.answer: Jill has fair hair but Jack has dark hair.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "i can hear an owl hoot at night"
questions.answer: I can hear an owl hoot at night.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "nan is sitting in the chair"
questions.answer: Nan is sitting in the chair.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "gurdeep had a chat with his dad"
questions.answer: Gurdeep had a chat with his dad.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses capital letters and punctuation correctly?
options: she has a pet snake named rosie
She has a pet snake named Rosie.
She has a pet Snake named rosie
she has a Pet snake named Rosie
questions.answer: She has a pet snake named Rosie.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correctly capitalized and punctuated?
options: marcus went to the mall last friday
Marcus went to the mall last Friday.
Marcus Went to the mall last friday.
marcus Went to the Mall last Friday
questions.answer: Marcus went to the mall last Friday.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "joanna is from florida"
Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "her mother likes to play tennis"
questions.answer: Her mother likes to play tennis.

questions.prompt: Which sentence needs a question mark at the end?
options: the boat sailed across the pacific ocean
i caught three fish at the lake
her mother likes to play tennis

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "the boat sailed across the pacific ocean"
questions.answer: The boat sailed across the Pacific Ocean.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "the united states is a big country"
questions.answer: The United States is a big country.

questions.prompt: Rewrite correctly with capitals and punctuation: "i caught three fish at the lake"
questions.answer: I caught three fish at the lake.

questions.prompt: Match each punctuation rule to what it describes.

questions.definition: Using a capital (uppercase) letter at the start of a sentence or for names.
questions.example: 'jill' becomes 'Jill' when it starts a sentence or is a name.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: Bow down to the king and queen

chapters.title: Reading Comprehension
chapters.snippet: From Jane Goodall's chimpanzees to a mysterious house called Cogston, great readers notice every clue in a story!

questions.prompt: According to the text, what animal does Jane Goodall know a lot about?
What did Jane's father give her when she was young?
Jane's stuffed chimpanzee was named ___.
What are chimpanzees, according to the text?
In 1957, Jane travelled to a country in Africa called...
The scientist Jane met in Africa, who knew a lot about chimpanzees, was named ___.
Why did Jane give the chimpanzees names instead of numbers?
options: It helped her get to know and understand them better
The other scientists told her to
The chimps chose their own names
questions.answer: It helped her get to know and understand them better

questions.prompt: What was special about the chimpanzee named David Greybeard?
What did Jane observe a chimpanzee doing with grass, which surprised scientists?
options: Using it like a spoon to eat insects
questions.answer: Using it like a spoon to eat insects

questions.prompt: In the story 'The Curse of Cogston House', who are the two main characters?
Where were Jack and Luca heading in the story?
In the story, fifty-foot-high ___ encased the crumbling walls of Cogston House.
How long ago had Cogston House stopped being inhabited, according to the text?
Match each fable to what happened in it.

pairs.right: A crow drops pebbles to raise the water level
A fox and a stork trick each other with narrow dishes
A bear is stung after disturbing a beehive

questions.prompt: In 'The Crow and the Pitcher', why could the Crow not drink the water at first?
options: The pitcher had a cap on
It was high with a narrow neck
There were worms in the water
questions.answer: It was high with a narrow neck

questions.prompt: In 'The Fox and the Stork', how did the Fox trick the Stork?
options: He served soup in a shallow dish the Stork could not eat from
He ate all the food before the Stork arrived
He invited the Stork but never showed up
questions.answer: He served soup in a shallow dish the Stork could not eat from

questions.prompt: In 'The Bear and the Bees', why did the Bear end up jumping into a pool of water?
options: To cool down from the heat
To escape the swarm of angry bees
questions.answer: To escape the swarm of angry bees

questions.definition: To figure something out using clues, even when it isn't stated directly — to 'read between the lines'.
questions.example: When Daisy's heart beat fast and she walked faster, we can infer that she felt scared.

questions.prompt: To infer a character's traits and feelings, the workbook teaches you to think 'FAST'. What does the 'F' stand for?
Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: Everyone had a story about Cogston House

chapters.title: Creative Writing: The Ocean & The Bicycle
chapters.snippet: Emma's shiny pink bicycle and the wide blue ocean both show how describing details makes a story come alive!

questions.prompt: According to 'The Ocean' passage, what color is the ocean water described as?
What do seagulls do over the ocean, according to the passage?
Complete the sentence from 'The Ocean': "There is soft sand along the ___."
Complete the sentence from 'The Ocean': "There are pretty ___ in the sand."
In 'The Bicycle', what color was Emma's new bicycle?
Who gave Emma the bicycle as a gift?
Where did Emma's uncle hide the bicycle to surprise her?
Complete the sentence: "When Emma looked behind the bush and saw the bicycle, she jumped for ___."
What did Emma do after she saw her new bicycle?
options: She gave her uncle a big hug
She rode away without saying thanks
She asked for a different color
questions.answer: She gave her uncle a big hug

questions.definition: Writing that uses vivid details — like colors, sounds, and feelings — to help the reader picture a scene.
questions.example: 'Bright blue water filled with waves' is descriptive writing about the ocean.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: The ocean is a great place to visit

questions.prompt: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.answer: She gave her uncle a big hug

questions.prompt: Match each story detail to the correct passage.
